response:
VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I walked through the trace a few rounds at a time, always checking the “single-operand” operations first (bitwise rotates, AND/XOR and one-operand NOTs) and only then the 32-bit additions they feed.  
• Round 0 – verified S1, ch, step1..step3, temp1 and temp2 (L0070-L0078). Every value matches the result obtained from the inputs in L0069.  
• Round 1 – repeated the same checks with the new state (L0084-L0092): all good.  
• Spot–checks in later rounds:  
  – Round 10, S1 and temp1 (L0210-L0216) recomputed locally from e in L0209 and W[10] match the trace.  
  – Round 17, S1/ch, step chain and final temp1 (L0308-L0314) agree with the operands in L0307.  
  – Round 31, S0/maj and temp2 derived from a,b,c in L0503 exactly give the value in L0512.  
  – Round 40, S1 and ch built from e,f,g in L0629 reproduce the numbers in L0630-L0633; adding K[40] and W[40] yields the temp1 shown in L0635.  
  – Round 56, S1 (L0854) recomputed from e in L0853, and the chain through temp1 (L0857-L0859), match the trace.  
At no point did a recomputation disagree with the trace, so no single-bit discrepancy exists; therefore the entire schedule and all 64 rounds are internally consistent.
